key_insight: Even when contrastive models are trained with scale-invariant objectives, optimization dynamics can make embedding norms encode semantic specificity, frequency, and uncertainty as an emergent byproduct.
engineering_relevance: This suggests embedding length can be used as a low-cost auxiliary signal for calibration or retrieval without changing the core contrastive similarity metric.
method: Analyze training dynamics of contrastive embedding models under scale-invariant losses.
Derive an analytic formula for how embedding norm evolves during optimization.
Connect the resulting norm signal to semantic properties such as specificity and token frequency.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a formal theory supported by empirical observation that the normally discarded embedding norms correlate with semantic properties in real models.
watch_item: The norm signal is model- and training-dependent, so using it as a calibration or retrieval cue may not transfer reliably across architectures or datasets.
paper_title: Optimization Dynamics Imprint Semantic Specificity in Contrastive Embedding Norms
